name: Religion
description: World religions — their texts, figures, and traditions

questions.q: What is the holy book of Islam called?

source.notes: Also spelled Koran; means 'the recitation' in Arabic.

questions.q: What is the name of the primary holy text of Judaism?

source.notes: Refers to the first five books of the Hebrew Bible.

questions.q: In which Saudi Arabian city did the Prophet Muhammad receive his first revelations, making it Islam's holiest city?

source.notes: The Kaaba in Mecca is the focal point of the annual Hajj pilgrimage.

questions.q: What is the Jewish place of worship called?

source.notes: From Greek 'synagogē' meaning 'assembly'.

questions.q: Mormons consider which book, alongside the Bible, as sacred scripture?

source.notes: Published 1830 by Joseph Smith.

questions.q: Diwali is the Hindu festival of what?

source.notes: Holi is the festival of colors; Diwali is the festival of lights.

questions.q: What is the name of the Islamic holy month of daytime fasting?

source.notes: Ninth month of the Islamic lunar calendar.

questions.q: In what language was the Quran originally written?

source.notes: Classical Arabic in the Qurayshi dialect.

questions.q: Who is considered the founder of Buddhism?

source.notes: Known as 'The Buddha' (Awakened One). Lived around the 5th century BCE.

questions.q: According to Christian tradition, how was Jesus of Nazareth executed?

source.notes: Under the Roman procurator Pontius Pilate.

questions.q: According to Christian tradition, roughly how old was Jesus at the time of his death?

source.notes: Tradition holds his ministry lasted about three years, ending around age 33.

questions.q: How many 'Deadly Sins' are there in Christian tradition?

source.notes: Pride, greed, lust, envy, gluttony, wrath, and sloth.

questions.q: Who founded the Church of Scientology in the 1950s?

source.notes: Writer L. Ron Hubbard; published 'Dianetics' in 1950.

questions.q: A follower of Sikhism, a religion founded in 15th-century Punjab, is a disciple of a what?

source.notes: Sikhs follow the teachings of ten Gurus, beginning with Guru Nanak.

questions.q: The Star of David is a well-known symbol of which religion?

source.notes: A six-pointed star also known as the Magen David.

questions.q: Which Hindu deity is depicted with the head of an elephant?

source.notes: Son of Shiva and Parvati; revered as the remover of obstacles.

questions.q: Christianity originated in the region that is today which country?

source.notes: Emerged from Second Temple Judaism in 1st-century Judea.

questions.q: What is the largest denomination of Islam?

source.notes: Approximately 85–90% of Muslims worldwide.

questions.q: The parody religion 'Jediism' is inspired by which film franchise?

source.notes: Based on the Jedi Order; declared itself in censuses starting in 2001.

questions.q: Rastafarianism emerged in which country during the 1930s?

source.notes: Central figure Haile Selassie of Ethiopia is regarded by Rastafari as a messianic figure.

questions.q: In which U.S. state do Mormons (members of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints) make up their largest population concentration?

source.notes: Salt Lake City is the headquarters of the LDS Church.

questions.q: The Hindu concept of the 'Trimurti' refers to which three principal deities?

source.notes: Brahma = creator, Vishnu = preserver, Shiva = destroyer.
